---
title: "Pincushion Moss (Leucobryum glaucum)"
slug: "leucobryum-glaucum"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/474803274/medium.jpeg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Pincushion Moss"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Bryopsida"
tax_order: "Dicranales"
tax_family: "Leucobryaceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Bryopsida", "Dicranales", "Leucobryaceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/474803274/medium.jpeg" alt="Pincushion Moss" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/luna101">
luna101</a> (CC BY-NC) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/264356711">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Bryopsida
-   **Order:** Dicranales
-   **Family:** Leucobryaceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=122658)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not Listed
-   **Rarity note:** Common species in Florida found in moist, shaded habitats.

## Natural history notes

A cushion-forming moss characterized by its dense, rounded mounds of pale green to bluish-green leaves. In Florida, it is found in moist, shaded habitats such as forests, rotting logs, and sandy soils. It grows year-round in suitable conditions and helps retain moisture and stabilize soil in forest ecosystems.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/122658-Leucobryum-glaucum)
-   [Local profile](https://explorer.natureserve.org/Taxon/ELEMENT_GLOBAL.2.125297/Leucobryum_glaucum)
